title: draba verna var. lucida
intro: Draba verna var. lucida is a variety of Draba verna, a flowering plant in the mustard family (Brassicaceae), native to the Arctic tundra and subarctic regions of Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine, believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, making it useful for treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high concentration of glucosinolates, which have been shown to have potential health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-cancer, and antimicrobial effects. Draba verna var. lucida is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and can be used externally as a poultice or ointment to aid in wound healing and skin conditions.
